title: baccharis tetroica
intro: Baccharis tetroica, also known as the sweet broom or button bush, is a shrub native to South America, particularly in the Andean region, known for its distinct white or yellowish flowers and feathery foliage. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Baccharis tetroica include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Baccharis tetroica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional folk medicine.
